nodes.label: AI as research starting point
nodes.grounding_span: AI was easier than doing a full library search on my own. It also helped me find a starting point

nodes.label: AI for clarity and conciseness in writing
nodes.grounding_span: I would definitely want to use AI in scientific writing such as abstracts, manuscripts, and conference submissions. I find it hard to use the best or most accurate terminology

nodes.label: AI use in core academic work (writing, analysis) as ethical concern
nodes.label_negative: AI use as acceptable academic tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think it felt unethical? Like it wouldn't be my own work or something. Maybe thats just my student brain conditioned to think of using AI sources as something that's similar to cheating

nodes.label: Thorough verification of AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I would always make sure that it produced valid links in the NCBI or NIH databases. I'd also read the articles myself

nodes.label: Research efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI was easier than doing a full library search on my own

nodes.label: Academic integrity
nodes.grounding_span: it wouldn't be my own work or something

nodes.label: Scientific communication clarity
nodes.grounding_span: I find it hard to use the best or most accurate terminology, and instead prefer to use my own writing voice that is often not as clear

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I would always make sure that it produced valid links in the NCBI or NIH databases

nodes.label: Positive appreciation of AI for efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI was easier than doing a full library search on my own

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI-generated writing
nodes.grounding_span: I think it felt unethical? Like it wouldn't be my own work or something

nodes.label: Aspirational stance toward AI for scientific communication
nodes.grounding_span: I would definitely want to use AI in scientific writing such as abstracts, manuscripts, and conference submissions

nodes.label: Cautious verification stance toward AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I would always make sure that it produced valid links in the NCBI or NIH databases

nodes.label: Verification-oriented — cross-checks AI outputs against other sources
nodes.grounding_span: I would always make sure that it produced valid links in the NCBI or NIH databases. I'd also read the articles myself

nodes.label: Boundary-setting and ethical deliberation
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI extensively during the background research... I wasn't at the point of using it to help me write just yet
